commodities.name: saint-paulin
terms: sliced, cut, shredded or grated cheese

sections.title: 2022 Amendment
sections.text:
The following amendment was made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the forty-fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.
Page  Location  Text in previous version   Text in amended version
5 Section 7.5 Labelling of non-retail containers
Information specified in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container, and in the absence of such a container, on the product itself. However, lot identification and the name and address may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.
The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

5 Section 7.5 Labelling of non-retail containers
Information specified in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container, and in the absence of such a container, on the product itself. sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This Standard applies to Saint -Paulin intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to Saint -Paulin intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to Saint -Paulin intended for direct consumption or for further processing in conformity with the description in Section 2 of this standard.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Saint-Paulin is a ripened firm/semi-hard cheese in conformity with the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978). The body has a near white or ivory through to light yellow or yellow colour and a firm-textured (when pressed by thumb) but flexible texture. Gas holes are generally absent, but few openings and splits are acceptable. The cheese is manufactured and sold with or without 1 a dry or slightly moist rind, which is hard, but elastic under thumb pressure, and which may be coated. For Saint-Paulin ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 1 week at 10–17 °C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzymes)may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Saint-Paulin intended for further processing need not exhibit the same degree of ripening when justified through technical and/or trade needs.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION
number=2
level=1
Saint-Paulin is a ripened firm/semi-hard cheese in conformity with the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978). The body has a near white or ivory through to light yellow or yellow colour and a firm-textured (when pressed by thumb) but flexible texture. Gas holes are generally absent, but few openings and splits are acceptable. The cheese is manufactured and sold with or without 1 a dry or slightly moist rind, which is hard, but elastic under thumb pressure, and which may be coated. For Saint-Paulin ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 1 week at 10–17 °C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzymes)may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Saint-Paulin intended for further processing need not exhibit the same degree of ripening when justified through technical and/or trade needs.

page_spans.text: Saint-Paulin is a ripened firm/semi-hard cheese in conformity with the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978). The body has a near white or ivory through to light yellow or yellow colour and a firm-textured (when pressed by thumb) but flexible texture. Gas holes are generally absent, but few openings and splits are acceptable. The cheese is manufactured and sold with or without 1 a dry or slightly moist rind, which is hard, but elastic under thumb pressure, and which may be coated. For Saint-Paulin ready for consumption, the ripening procedure to develop flavour and body characteristics is normally from 1 week at 10–17 °C depending on the extent of maturity required. Alternative ripening conditions (including the addition of ripening enhancing enzymes)may be used, provided the cheese exhibits similar physical, biochemical and sensory properties as those achieved by the previously stated ripening procedure. Saint-Paulin intended for further processing need not exhibit the same degree of ripening when justified through technical and/or trade needs.

sections.title: Permitted ingredients
sections.text: – Starter cultures of harmless lactic acid and/ or flavour producing bacteria and cultures of other harmless micro-organisms; – Rennet or other safe and suitable coagulating enzymes; – Sodium chloride and potassium chloride as a salt substitute; – Potable water; – Safe and suitable enzymes to enhance the ripening process; – Safe and suitable processing aids; – Rice, corn and potato flours and starches: Notwithstanding the provisions in the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978), these substances can be used in the same function as anti -caking agents for treatment of the surface of cut, sliced, and shredded products only, provided they are added only in amounts functionally necessary as governed by Good Manufacturing Practi ce, taking into account any use of the anti-caking agents listed in Section 4.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Only those additives classes indicated as justified in the table below may be used for the product categories specified. Anticaking agents, colours and preservatives used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 01.6.2.1 (Ripened cheese, includes rind) and only certain acidity regulators and anticaking agents in Table 3 are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard. Additive functional class: Justified use Cheese mass Surface/rind treatment Colours: X(a) – Bleaching agents: – – Acidity regulators: X – Stabilizers: – – Thickeners: – – Emulsifiers: – – Antioxidants: – – Preservatives: X X Foaming agents: – – Anti–caking agents: – X(b) (a) Only to obtain the colour characteristics, as described in Section 2. (b) For the surface of sliced, cut, shredded or grated cheese, only. X The use of additives belonging to the class is technologically justified. – The use of additives belonging to the class is not technologically justified.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the CAC.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: LABELLING
sections.text: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:
sections.retrieval_text:
LABELLING
number=7
level=1

page_spans.text: In addition to the provisions of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre -packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and the General Standard for the Use of Dairy Terms (CXS 206-1999), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name Saint -Paulin may be applied in accordance with Section 4.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), provided that the product is in conformity with this Standard. Where customary in the country of retail sale, alternative spelling may be used. The use of the name is an option that may be chosen only if the cheese complies with this standard. Where the name is not used for a cheese that complies with this standard, the naming provisions of the General Standard for Cheese (CXS 283-1978) apply.

sections.title: Country of origin
sections.text: The country of origin (which means the country of manufacture, not the country in which the name originated) shall be declared. When the product undergoes substantial transformation in a second country, the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling.

sections.title: Declaration of milkfat content
sections.text: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.

sections.title: Date marking
sections.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information, provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.

sentences.original: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

classification.sentence: It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex texts such as Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
classification.function: Recommendation/Requirement
classification.pattern: It is recommended that <entity> be <action> in accordance with <standards>; the <entity> should comply with <criteria> established according to <guidelines>.

components.entity_1: the product covered by the provisions of this standard
components.standards: the appropriate sections of General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004), other relevant Codex texts
components.criteria_basis: Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence expresses recommended obligations with modal verbs and recommendations, linking preparation, handling, and compliance to specific standards and microbiological criteria. There is no exact match in the known templates, and the sentence contains a compound structure with multiple recommended actions tied to standards and guidance. The modality is obligation as the core function is prescribing recommended practices that imply compliance requirements.
notes: The sentence includes multiple interconnected recommendations making it complex; no exact prior template available, so a new structural pattern reflecting recommendation and compliance obligation is generated.
Use of "It is recommended" and "should" conveys a normative obligation tone rather than permission or mere suggestion.

notes: The sentence mixes a conditional context with a definitional obligation, which may overlap partially with templates on conditions or origin definitions but no strong match is found.

classification.sentence: When the product undergoes substantial transformation in a second country, the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling.
classification.function: Definition of applicable origin rule under condition
classification.pattern: Conditional clause (when [event]) + main clause stating obligation to consider a country as origin for labelling

components.condition: When the product undergoes substantial transformation in a second country
components.obligation: the country in which the transformation is performed shall be considered to be the country of origin for the purpose of labelling

classification.justification: The sentence combines a condition introduced by 'When' with an obligation about treating the transformation country as the country of origin for labelling; this pattern does not strongly match existing templates but clearly expresses a conditional obligation defining origin labelling.
notes: The sentence mixes a conditional context with a definitional obligation, which may overlap partially with templates on conditions or origin definitions but no strong match is found.

sentences.original: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.

classification.sentence: The milk fat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter, or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label, provided that the number of servings is stated.
classification.function: Prescriptive requirement with allowable alternative methods and condition
classification.pattern: Subject shall be declared in an acceptable manner with alternative formats enumerated and a condition qualifying one option

components.subject: The milk fat content
components.manner: in a manner found acceptable in the country of retail sale
alternative_formats: (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter
(iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label
components.condition: provided that the number of servings is stated

classification.justification: The sentence expresses an obligation to declare the milk fat content, specifying allowed alternative expression formats and adds a condition linked to one format. This specific structure with multiple alternatives and a qualifying condition does not strongly match any known template, thus classified as a new pattern. The presence of enumeration and conditional clause within an obligation sentence supports this classification.
notes: Sentence contains an obligation with enumerated alternative formats and a conditional qualifier, a structure not common in simple obligation templates.
Potential ambiguity in whether the condition applies only to the third format or all alternatives, noted but not altering classification.

sentences.original: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information, provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.

classification.sentence: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information, provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer.
classification.function: Granting Conditional Permission
classification.pattern: [Exception clause], [Permission granted], [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.exception_clause: Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 4.7.1 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)
components.permission_granted: the date of manufacture may be declared instead of the minimum durability information
components.condition: provided that the product is not intended to be purchased as such by the final consumer

classification.justification: The sentence grants permission conditionally, with an initial exception clause. It does not fit known templates but clearly expresses conditional permission with an exception clause and condition, thus a new pattern is created reflecting this structure.
notes: The sentence includes a legal exception phrase and a conditional restriction, making its structure more complex than standard templates.

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

sentences.original: For checking the compliance with this Standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this Standard, shall be used.

classification.sentence: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.
classification.function: Prescriptive requirement
classification.pattern: Introductory phrase indicating purpose + main clause prescribing required methods and reference standards with modal verb 'shall'

components.purpose: For checking the compliance with this standard
components.required_items: methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions in this standard

classification.justification: The sentence prescribes an obligation to use specified methods for compliance checking with normative modality 'shall'. The sentence structure includes an introductory purpose phrase followed by a prescriptive main clause. No known templates match closely, so a new pattern reflecting this structure is created.
notes: The sentence combines normative obligation with purpose indication in a complex sentence; no direct template found in catalog.

sentences.original: The designations “Petit Saint-Paulin” and “Mini Saint-Paulin” should be used when the cheese complies with the provisions for dimensions and weights (1.2).

classification.sentence: The designations “Petit Saint-Paulin” and “Mini Saint-Paulin” should be used when the cheese complies with the provisions for dimensions and weights (1.2).
classification.function: Obligation conditioned on compliance
classification.pattern: Obligation statement ('should be used') + temporal/conditional clause ('when the cheese complies...')

components.Obligation: The designations “Petit Saint-Paulin” and “Mini Saint-Paulin” should be used
components.Condition: when the cheese complies with the provisions for dimensions and weights (1.2)

classification.justification: Obligation sentence with a condition introduced by 'when' giving a temporal constraint; partial match to obligation templates with conditionals.
notes: Use of 'should' indicates normative obligation rather than mere permission.
Contains condition but formula resembles known obligation-conditional patterns.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR SAINT-PAULIN
pages.summary: A página contém apenas o título do documento e o cabeçalho padrão com a identificação do código e datas de adoção/revisão. Não há seções ou texto estrutural nesta página para mapear.

A página contém um título em caixa alta '2022 Amendment' considerado nível 1 e um único nó textual explicativo associado a este título. Há um cabeçalho repetitivo 'CXS 271-1968  2', que foi excluído do texto dos nós. Não foram identificadas outras seções ou subdivisões nesta página.

A página apresenta três seções principais numeradas 1, 2 e 3. A seção 3 é subdividida em três subseções numeradas 3.1, 3.2 e 3.3. Cada seção e subseção tem texto explicativo associado. A seção 3 tem continuação na próxima página (continues_on_next_page=true). O cabeçalho repetitivo da página foi identificado e excluído do texto dos nós. Não há título principal do documento visível nesta página.

A página contém as seções numeradas 4 a 7, com uma subseção 7.1. Cada seção tem texto explicativo associado, sem títulos de nível superior visíveis na página. Não há continuidade de conteúdo entre páginas para essas seções. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e excluído do texto dos nós. A estrutura está clara para uso em RAG.

A página apresenta subtítulos da seção 7 (7.2 a 7.5) e o início da seção 8, cada um com seu texto explicativo associado. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e excluído do texto dos nós. A estrutura hierárquica está clara e sem continuidade para a próxima página.

A página apresenta um apêndice com informações adicionais numeradas e hierarquizadas em níveis claros. O título principal do apêndice está em caixa alta e sem numeração, sendo nível 1. As seções 1, 2 e 3 são nível 1 por conterem numeração simples, com subseções 1.1, 1.2, 2.1 e 2.2 no nível 2. O texto das seções está associado corretamente, sem incluir o cabeçalho repetitivo. Não há continuidade da página anterior nem para a próxima página.
